prompt:
Security/compliance ticket. Audit dinged us because Terraform Enterprise workspace team access is granted ad hoc in the UI and never revoked — we found a contractor team that still had admin on the payments workspace six months after offboarding. Team access is going into code review: a desired map lives in git, a reconciler makes TFE match it. The repo already has tfe_client.rb, our small working /api/v2 transport (bearer auth, JSON:API media type, returns [status, parsed-body] instead of raising on non-2xx). It's in production; don't change its behavior. The reconciler is the missing piece.

Do the reading before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to confirm the CURRENT official Terraform Enterprise API documentation on developer.hashicorp.com — the API overview page (auth, media type, page[number]/page[size] pagination with meta.pagination, error documents) and the Team Access API reference. Pay attention to the 404 story: TFE deliberately answers 404 for records you're not allowed to see, not just missing ones — the docs even spell out 'Team access not found or user unauthorized to perform action' — and the reconciler's behavior is built around that. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json, provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both protected fixtures. If the live official docs contradict them, report the conflict explicitly in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

The feature — new file team_access_reconciler.rb defining module TeamAccess:

- TeamAccess.fetch(client, workspace_id, page_size: 20) — list the workspace's team access via the documented list endpoint: the required workspace filter parameter on EVERY page, page[number]/page[size], following meta.pagination next-page until null. Returns an array of {"id", "team_id", "access"} hashes in server order — team_id comes from the team relationship linkage, not from any attribute.
- TeamAccess::APIError (status + parsed errors array, message carrying the detail) for unexpected non-2xx responses, and TeamAccess::NotFoundOrUnauthorized < APIError for the masked 404s — its message must convey BOTH possibilities (not found or unauthorized). A 404 on the list raises NotFoundOrUnauthorized.
- TeamAccess.plan(current, desired) — desired is a hash of team id => access level. Validate levels against the documented set (read/plan/write/admin/custom) with ArgumentError before anything else. Returns {"add" => [{team_id, access}], "change" => [{id, team_id, from, to}], "remove" => [{id, team_id, access}]}, each list sorted by team_id; teams whose level already matches generate nothing.
- TeamAccess.format_plan(plan) — the deterministic dry-run lines, adds then changes then removes: '+ <team> <access>', '~ <team> <from> -> <to> (<id>)', '- <team> <access> (<id>)'.
- TeamAccess.apply(client, workspace_id, plan) — adds then changes then removes, in plan order, with the documented JSON:API bodies: create POSTs data.type team-workspaces with the access attribute and team/workspace relationship linkages (types teams/workspaces); change PATCHes the per-record path repeating the id in data.id with only the access attribute; remove DELETEs with no body. Returns report lines 'add <team> <access>', 'change <team> <from>-><to>', 'remove <team>'. The 404 rules from docs/contract.json: a 404 on PATCH or DELETE means that record vanished or became invisible mid-reconcile — record 'skip <team> (missing or unauthorized)' and keep going; a 404 on POST means the team or workspace itself is invisible — abort with NotFoundOrUnauthorized. Any other non-2xx raises APIError.
- TeamAccess.reconcile(client, workspace_id, desired, dry_run: false) — fetch + plan + format, applying only when dry_run is false; returns {"plan", "lines", "report"}. A dry run issues zero write requests.

Acceptance is test_team_access.rb (minitest, loopback fake TFE, dummy token). Run: ruby test_team_access.rb — until green. test_team_access.rb and everything under docs/ are protected; tfe_client.rb stays as is.

research.interface: Terraform Enterprise REST API v2 (/api/v2, JSON:API): team access (team-workspaces) endpoints (stable)

official_sources.title: API documentation for Terraform Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: /api/v2 base path, Authorization: Bearer token auth, application/vnd.api+json JSON:API media type, page[number]/page[size] pagination with meta.pagination (current-page, prev-page, next-page, total-pages, total-count), JSON:API errors array shape, and 404 responses masking unauthorized resources

official_sources.title: /team-workspaces API reference for Terraform Enterprise (Team Access)
official_sources.used_for: GET /team-workspaces with required filter[workspace][id] and pagination; the team-workspaces resource type, access attribute values read/plan/write/admin/custom, and team/workspace relationships; POST create body; PATCH/DELETE /team-workspaces/:id; and the 404 'Team access not found or user unauthorized to perform action' masking semantics

research.language_rationale: There is no official HashiCorp Ruby SDK for the TFE API; the documented integration surface is the raw JSON:API REST contract at /api/v2, and Ruby's stdlib net/http plus json (with minitest for acceptance) is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to implement a small reconciler against it.
